task_context.description: This is a comparative detector design and simulation study for electromagnetic calorimetry in high-energy physics. The task involves designing and evaluating three different homogeneous crystal calorimeter configurations using different scintillating crystal materials (PbWO4, BGO, and CsI) for a future electron-positron collider experiment. The study requires running Geant4 Monte Carlo simulations to characterize detector performance across multiple energy points, measuring key physics parameters like energy resolution, shower containment, and spatial resolution. The task combines detector engineering design with systematic performance benchmarking to make an evidence-based material selection recommendation for the FCC-ee electromagnetic calorimeter.
task_context.goal: Select the optimal crystal material for an electromagnetic calorimeter at a future e+e- collider by comparing the detector performance characteristics of PbWO4, BGO, and CsI crystals
constraints: Use homogeneous calorimeter design (no absorber material)
Limit to 3 configurations maximum (one per material)
Use tractable simulation parameters for computational feasibility
Test only with electrons (e-) in particle gun
Use specified geometry options: box, homogeneous_slab, or projective_tower topology
